CRITICAL_CORRECTION.issue: ALL numeric values in config.xbr are 64-bit IEEE 754 doubles, NOT 32-bit floats.
CRITICAL_CORRECTION.previous_bug: The original build_registry.py read bytes 8-11 as float32 (struct.unpack '<f' at offset+8), producing WRONG values for every property.
CRITICAL_CORRECTION.correct_read: Read 8 bytes at record_offset + 4 as little-endian double: struct.unpack_from('<d', data, record_offset + 4)
CRITICAL_CORRECTION.correct_write: Write 8 bytes at record_offset + 4 as little-endian double: struct.pack_into('<d', data, record_offset + 4, value)

patching_guide.settings_foo_note: settings_foo uses 48-byte GROUPS: [meta_record(16)][VALUE_record(16)][term_record(16)]. Write double at group_offset + 16 + 4.

critters_walking.description: Per-entity AI movement and combat behaviour parameters.

settings_foo.description: Global player settings: fuel capacities, HP. Only 6 properties have binary data; remaining 15 (max_hp, fall damage, level-up bonuses) are NOT in config.xbr.
settings_foo.note: Only 6 properties (initial_fuel through initial_hp) have binary records. Properties max_hp through fall_min_velocity exist only as string-table names; their values must be hardcoded in the XBE executable.
settings_foo.missing_properties_note: max_hp, lev2_obs, lev2_hp, lev3_obs, lev3_hp, lev4_obs, lev4_hp, fall_height_1-4, fall_damage_1-3, fall_min_velocity have NO binary data in config.xbr. Their default values are likely in the XBE at addresses listed in xbe_property_addresses.

damage.description: Enemy-specific damage overrides. 11 entities with 8 patchable double values each. Base damage types (norm_1-4, water_1-3, fire_1-3, etc.) are NOT stored as records — only enemy-specific overrides have custom numeric data.
damage.note: Entity identification is by position (name_length matching). String references use an opaque offset format not yet fully decoded.

critters_flocking.description: Flocking/boid behaviour for ambient group critters. 25 properties per entity.

armor_properties.description: Armor type definitions. Only 3 entities decoded from binary records (no_armor_1, earth_armor_1, ice_armor_1). Remaining 16 armor types likely defined via string-table-only format.
armor_properties.note: Record-to-property mapping is approximate. Only 3 of 19 armor types have binary data; the rest use the string-keyed format.

critters_critter_data.description: Per-entity base stats (speeds, attack range, drops, collision). Uses keyed/string-reference format. All 107 entities' values are stored as string references, not variant records. NOT directly patchable via offset overwrites.
critters_critter_data.note: This section uses a keyed format where all values (including numeric ones) are stored as string references into an internal string pool. The engine's config parser reads these using a different code path than the flat variant-record sections. Values cannot be patched by overwriting bytes at a fixed offset — the string pool and reference table would both need modification.

critters_damage.description: Per-entity HP and elemental resistance multipliers. Uses keyed/string-reference format with ~100 entities and 39 properties. Entity names + property names are in the string table. NOT directly patchable via offset overwrites.
critters_damage.note: This section uses a keyed format where all values (including numeric ones) are stored as string references into an internal string pool. The engine's config parser reads these using a different code path than the flat variant-record sections. Values cannot be patched by overwriting bytes at a fixed offset — the string pool and reference table would both need modification.

magic.description: Magic ability definitions (16 types). Uses keyed format with string refs for Sequence, Type, Damage, Armor, Auto, Effect, L2/L3 Effect. All values are string-encoded. NOT directly patchable.
magic.note: This section uses a keyed format where all values (including numeric ones) are stored as string references into an internal string pool. The engine's config parser reads these using a different code path than the flat variant-record sections. Values cannot be patched by overwriting bytes at a fixed offset — the string pool and reference table would both need modification.

attacks_anims.description: Attack animation definitions (~20 attack types). Uses keyed format with string refs for animation names, damage/fuel multipliers (as strings), timing values. NOT directly patchable.
attacks_anims.note: This section uses a keyed format where all values (including numeric ones) are stored as string references into an internal string pool. The engine's config parser reads these using a different code path than the flat variant-record sections. Values cannot be patched by overwriting bytes at a fixed offset — the string pool and reference table would both need modification.
